evals.prompt: Write a doc explaining why our system uses event sourcing instead of CRUD.
evals.expected_output: An understanding-oriented explanation covering background, rationale, trade-offs, and alternatives.
expectations: Discusses the 'why' — context, rationale, and trade-offs
Avoids step-by-step instructions and exhaustive reference tables
Emits MIF frontmatter type: semantic and passes mif-validate --level 1

evals.prompt: This explanation doc has turned into a numbered setup procedure. Fix the mode.
evals.expected_output: Identifies how-to content inside an explanation and refocuses it on understanding.
expectations: Flags the procedural/how-to content as out of mode
Recommends moving steps to a how-to and linking it
Rewrites the content as discursive understanding-oriented prose

evals.prompt: What's the difference between an explanation and a reference doc in Diataxis?
evals.expected_output: Explains explanation is understanding-oriented (the why) and reference is information-oriented (the what).
expectations: Notes explanation is discursive while reference is structured/neutral
Gives an example topic for each

evals.prompt: Promote our two-view design rationale to MIF Level 3 so an agent can tell if it's current, who authored it, and what it cites.
evals.expected_output: An understanding-oriented explanation whose frontmatter carries temporal, provenance, citations, and typed relationships, passing mif-validate --level 3.
expectations: Keeps the body discursive and understanding-oriented (still an explanation, not a how-to)
Adds temporal (validFrom/ttl/recordedAt), provenance (sourceType + trustLevel + wasAttributedTo), citations[], and typed relationships[]
Every metadata value is genuine for the document, not placeholder filler
Passes mif-validate --level 3 with a lossless markdown<->JSON-LD round-trip
